---
title: ""
format: html
page-layout: full
---

<style>

body{
margin:0;
background:#f4e4c1;
font-family:"Times New Roman", serif;
}

.top-banner{
position:fixed;
top:0;
left:0;
width:100%;
background:#5c3a1e;
border-bottom:3px solid #d4a84f;
padding:8px 0;
text-align:center;
z-index:9999;
}

.top-banner a{
color:#ffd86b;
text-decoration:none;
font-weight:bold;
font-size:15px;
letter-spacing:1.5px;
margin:0 22px;
text-transform:uppercase;
}

.content{
margin-top:100px;
width:90%;
max-width:1500px;
margin:auto;
color:#3a2a1a;
}

.course{
display:flex;
gap:60px;
margin-top:30px;
align-items:flex-start;
}

.course-text{
flex:1;
font-size:18px;
line-height:1.7;
max-width:650px;
}

.slideshow{
flex:1.4;
max-width:900px;
height:560px;
position:relative;
overflow:hidden;
border-radius:14px;
}

.slide{
position:absolute;
top:0;
left:0;
width:100%;
height:100%;
object-fit:cover;
opacity:0;
transition:opacity .7s ease;
}

.slide.active{
opacity:1;
}

.arrow{
position:absolute;
top:50%;
transform:translateY(-50%);
font-size:45px;
background:rgba(0,0,0,.45);
color:white;
padding:10px 20px;
cursor:pointer;
border-radius:8px;
}

.left{left:18px;}
.right{right:18px;}

</style>


<div class="top-banner">

<a href="index.html">HOME</a>
<a href="portfolio.html">PORTFOLIO</a>
<a href="upperdivs.html">UPPER DIVS</a>

</div>


<div class="content">

# Upper Division Coursework

One of the most exciting parts of studying biology is getting to see how the ideas we learn in lecture actually appear in the real world. Many of my upper division classes combine traditional lectures with hands-on experiences, field observations, and opportunities to interact directly with organisms in their natural environments.

Another important component of modern biology is the ability to work with data. Courses like **BIOL 101** focus on developing computational and analytical skills that allow biologists to interpret datasets, visualize biological patterns, and communicate scientific results clearly.


## BIOL 101 – Computational Biology and Data Skills

BIOL 101 focuses on developing computational and data analysis skills that are increasingly important in modern biology. In this course we learned how coding can be used to analyze biological datasets, visualize patterns, and communicate scientific findings more effectively.

Using tools such as **R and Quarto**, we practiced organizing datasets, creating figures, and interpreting results from biological studies.

**Project File 3**  
<a href="Kristine-Santos-Unit-3-Project.html" target="_blank">View My BIOL 101 Website Project</a>


## BIOL 163 – Vertebrate Natural History

<div class="course">

<div class="course-text">

Vertebrate Natural History explores how vertebrates behave and survive in their natural environments.

A highlight of the course was participating in overnight field trips to the **Mojave Desert** and the **James Reserve in Idyllwild**, where we conducted nighttime surveys and observed animals such as **owls and snakes**.

</div>

<div class="slideshow" id="bioSlides">

<img src="IMG_7404.jpg" class="slide active">
<img src="BIOL163(2).jpg" class="slide">
<img src="BIOL163(3).jpg" class="slide">
<img src="BIOL163(4).jpg" class="slide">
<img src="IMG_7389.jpg" class="slide">

<div class="arrow left" onclick="changeSlide('bioSlides',-1)">❮</div>
<div class="arrow right" onclick="changeSlide('bioSlides',1)">❯</div>

</div>

</div>


## ENTM 100 – General Entomology

<div class="course">

<div class="course-text">

General Entomology explores insect diversity and anatomy.

Building our own insect collections felt a bit like **catching Pokémon**, except instead of Pokéballs we used nets and specimen boxes.

Each insect was preserved, labeled, and identified using morphological traits such as wings, antennae, and body segments.

</div>

<div class="slideshow" id="entSlides">

<img src="ENTM01.JPG" class="slide active">
<img src="ENTM02.jpg" class="slide">
<img src="ENTM03.jpg" class="slide">

<div class="arrow left" onclick="changeSlide('entSlides',-1)">❮</div>
<div class="arrow right" onclick="changeSlide('entSlides',1)">❯</div>

</div>

</div>


## BPSC 104 – Plant Diversity

Plant Diversity explores how plants evolved and how botanists classify them based on morphological traits such as reproductive structures and vascular systems.

Looking at plants through this perspective changes how you see them in everyday environments.


</div>


<script>

function changeSlide(id, step){

let slideshow = document.getElementById(id)
let slides = slideshow.querySelectorAll(".slide")

let index = 0

slides.forEach((slide,i)=>{
if(slide.classList.contains("active")) index=i
})

slides[index].classList.remove("active")

index += step

if(index<0) index=slides.length-1
if(index>=slides.length) index=0

slides[index].classList.add("active")

}

</script>
